title: Package workload-unit wall excludes setup that selected timing includes
summary: The package executor records selected timing with setup included for non-prepared runs, but starts process-wall timing only after createRuntime() has already finished. That makes workloadUnitWall and selectedTiming measure different boundaries, so the reported full-wall view can be smaller than the selected operation timing and host-gap analysis becomes invalid.
impact: Package-surface compare reports can understate workload wall time, misstate selected-gap/host-overhead buckets, and violate the repo's requirement that timing scopes remain symmetric and interpretable.

title: Package harness uses a Doe-only private dispatch fast path
summary: The shared package harness conditionally calls pass._dispatchBound() instead of the standard WebGPU sequence setPipeline()/setBindGroup()/dispatchWorkgroups(). The Doe package defines this private fused entrypoint in-repo, so the benchmark permits provider-dependent API call shape instead of enforcing identical WebGPU calls across sides.
impact: Package-surface results can include harness-side optimization on Doe that the comparison side does not receive, which breaks the user's requested identical-call-shape audit and makes encode-time differences harder to interpret as implementation-only.

title: Workload-unit wall normalization multiplies commandRepeat and timingDivisor
summary: Timing interpretation normalizes elapsedMs and trace-meta totals by commandRepeat * timingNormalizationDivisor. Several comparable workloads already encode one workload unit by setting both repeat and divisor to the same cycle count, so this model can over-normalize wall metrics and host-overhead buckets relative to the stated workload unit.
impact: Compare reports can present wall and host-overhead stats on the wrong unit for repeated-cycle workloads, which makes cross-workload reasoning and fairness auditing less trustworthy.

title: Run receipts drop sample-level timing metadata needed for strict post-hoc auditing
summary: Run-artifact serialization strips the per-sample timing object, then compare-from-artifacts reconstructs only a partial timing view from receipt-wide normalization and traceMeta. That loses commandRepeat, timingSelectionPolicy provenance, and upload ignore-first source details that strict comparability checks expect.
impact: Post-hoc compare from run receipts cannot reliably reproduce sample normalization or enforce some timing-policy and upload ignore-first invariants, weakening the benchmark system's audit trail.

title: Command-plan fallback silently fabricates comparable compute metadata
summary: When the package executor is given a command-style plan, it rewrites it into a neutral plan with hard-coded executorId, domain='compute', comparable=true, and a fixed timing contract. That is a hidden contract rewrite rather than an explicit workload declaration.
impact: This path makes the harness harder to reason about and could silently upgrade directional or non-compute compatibility artifacts into apparently comparable package workloads if it is used outside the current narrow test surface.
